mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate sustained above 5% represents a restrictive monetary stance that raises the discount rate on equity cash flows and tightens financial conditions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as valuations compress.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or high-rate periods show no significant underperformance versus low-rate periods (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points from its 12-month trailing low (a Sahm-rule-like threshold), this signals the onset of labor market deterioration that historically precedes recessions, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations fall.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp above its trailing 12-month minimum
failure_condition: Following triggered instances, S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of cases, or Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The joint condition of elevated nominal FEDFUNDS and still-high CPIAUCSL implies a positive but narrow real policy rate that squeezes real activity without signaling imminent easing, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days when both series remain jointly elevated relative to trend.
failure_condition: Joint high-rate/high-inflation regime shows forward 20d returns statistically indistinguishable from unconditional baseline (p > 0.05), or Rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, a decline in HOUST reflects rate-sensitive housing demand contraction that leads broader economic slowdown, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days; this relationship is expected to be weak or absent outside tightening regimes.
failure_condition: HOUST declines fail to precede negative 60d SPX returns in tightening regimes with hit rate below 55%, or the effect is equally strong in easing regimes (regime interaction not significant).

mechanism_narrative: A positive month-over-month trend in INDPRO signals expanding industrial output and resilient corporate earnings capacity, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as short-term momentum in real activity is priced into equities.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO momentum and forward 5d returns stays below 0.02 over any rolling 1-year window, or positive INDPRO trend periods show no significant outperformance (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS month-over-month growth exceeds 0.5%, it signals robust consumer spending momentum that supports corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as consumption-driven earnings beats materialize.
failure_condition: Triggered instances of strong RSAFS growth produce positive 20d returns in less than 55% of cases, or Rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous deceleration in PAYEMS monthly growth combined with a rising UNRATE indicates a coincident weakening of both labor demand and supply-side slack absorption, jointly signaling recessionary risk that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days beyond what either series predicts alone.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average growth < 100K and UNRATE rising for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Joint-trigger instances fail to produce negative 60d returns more often than either single-series trigger alone, or interaction term contributes no incremental Rank IC (< 0.02) versus univariate baselines.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS plateaus for at least 3 consecutive months after a prior tightening sequence, markets interpret this as a policy pause near the terminal rate, reducing rate-hike uncertainty and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days, but only during late-cycle tightening pauses rather than mid-cycle hikes.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS unchanged for 3+ consecutive months following a prior hiking sequence
failure_condition: During identified pause periods, S&P 500 20d forward returns are not significantly higher than during active-hiking periods (p > 0.05), or Rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
